vocab.meaning: truyền thêm ánh sáng vào (không gian)
vocab.example: I purchased a large convex mirror specifically to infuse light into a rather dim and gloomy corner of my hallway.

vocab.meaning: một món đồ độc lập (không cần đi kèm bộ)
vocab.example: I didn't want a full vanity set, so I just bought an elegant standalone piece that sits perfectly on my dresser.
I’m quite picky with mirrors; I usually look for those with a minimalist aesthetic and no visible frame.

vocab.meaning: bề mặt không tì vết (hoàn hảo)
vocab.example: When buying a mirror, I always inspect it closely to ensure it has an unblemished surface with no scratches or silvering issues.

vocab.meaning: lựa chọn/quyết định chọn cái gì
vocab.example: I opted for a fog-resistant mirror for my bathroom renovation to avoid the hassle of wiping it down after every shower.

vocab.meaning: kích thước được đặt làm riêng
vocab.example: Since my dressing room has an odd layout, I had to commission a mirror with bespoke dimensions to fit the wall perfectly.
I recently invested in a wall-mounted magnifying mirror which has been a game-changer for my daily skincare routine.
I believe that the mirrors you choose to buy can really reflect the personality and taste of the homeowner.
I was willing to pay a premium for a high-end mirror that offers high-definition clarity without any color distortion.

vocab.meaning: đặc tính giúp mở rộng không gian
vocab.example: Interior designers often recommend buying mirrors for their space-enhancing properties in small urban apartments.
